Home Docs API Examples

SeaPortal API Reference

This document describes the SeaPortal extraction library API.

Installation

go get github.com/seaportal/seaportal

Quick Start

Import the library and extract content from any URL:

import "github.com/seaportal/seaportal/pkg/extract"

result, err := extract.ExtractURL("https://example.com")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
fmt.Println(result.Content)

Functions

ExtractURL

func ExtractURL(url string) (*Result, error)

Extracts clean content from the given URL. Returns a Result containing the markdown content, metadata, and classification.

Parameters

  • url - The URL to extract content from

Returns

  • *Result - The extraction result
  • error - Any error encountered

ExtractHTML

func ExtractHTML(html string, baseURL string) (*Result, error)

Extracts content from raw HTML. Useful when you already have the HTML and don't need to fetch it.

Classify

func Classify(result Result) PageProfile

Classifies a page based on extraction result. Returns the page type (static, SSR, hydrated, SPA, dynamic, blocked).

Types

Result

type Result struct {
    Content     string   // Extracted markdown
    Title       string   // Page title
    Confidence  int      // Extraction confidence (0-100)
    Length      int      // Content length in bytes
    IsSPA       bool     // True if SPA detected
    IsBlocked   bool     // True if bot detection triggered
    SPASignals  []string // SPA indicators found
}

PageProfile

type PageProfile struct {
    Class       PageClass         // static, ssr, hydrated, spa, dynamic, blocked
    Outcome     ExtractionOutcome // extract, extract-with-warning, fail-fast, needs-browser
    Trustworthy bool              // Whether extraction is reliable
}

Configuration

SeaPortal can be configured via environment variables or programmatically.

Environment Variables

VariableDescriptionDefault
SEAPORTAL_TIMEOUTHTTP timeout30s
SEAPORTAL_CACHE_DIRCache directory~/.seaportal/cache
SEAPORTAL_USER_AGENTCustom user agentSeaPortal/1.0

Examples

See the examples directory for complete working examples.

SeaPortal Documentation v1.0
